read.lens: the diss as cultural eviction notice
read.take: They not like us draws a line and puts everyone on one side or the other. The charges are specific: the entourage with criminal cases, a warning to hide your little sister from him, the Atlanta trips for credibility he cannot earn at home. You not a colleague, you a fuckin' colonizer.
read.contradiction: The line drawn to evict him is what makes everyone else belong.

arc.label: The Sport
arc.scene: He enters already having won. There is no case to build, only one to read out, and he takes his time with it because winning this easily is its own kind of fun.

arc.label: The Us
arc.scene: Four words split the room and then fill one half of it. Saying them is the membership, and the man being named is the only one who cannot.

arc.label: The History
arc.scene: The accusation stops being personal. It reaches back centuries, to who built what and who only ever visited, and lands somewhere a diss cannot reach. What he wanted could never be bought, only inherited.

lines.line: The industry can hate me, fuck 'em all and they mama
Sometimes you gotta pop out and show niggas
Tryna strike a chord and it's probably A minor
No, you not a colleague, you a fuckin' colonizer

read.texture: A West Coast bounce that struts without breaking a sweat, where the menace lives entirely in how relaxed it sounds.
